quality.rationale: 线性引力规范固定标准推导，含多步张量缩并与达朗贝尔方程求解，逻辑严密且可平移至任意微扰背景。

origin.question: Problem 13.14. In linearized theory one often uses the "trace reversed" form of the metric perturbations $\overline{\mathbf{h}}_{\alpha}\beta \equiv \mathbf{h}_{\alpha}\beta -\frac{1}{2}\eta_{\alpha \beta}\mathbf{h}_{\sigma}^{\sigma}$ . Show that there always exists a gauge transformation to the "Lorentz gauge", in which the divergence of $\overline{\mathbf{h}}_{\alpha}\beta$ vanishes. Is this gauge transformation unique?
origin.answer: 存在满足波动方程 $\square\xi_{\alpha}=\partial^{\beta}\overline{h}_{\alpha\beta}$ 的四维矢量函数族 $\xi_{\alpha}$，选取其一即可实现Lorentz规范；该规范变换不唯一，可叠加齐次波动方程 $\square\xi_{\alpha}=0$ 的任意解。
origin.solution:
Solution 13.14. Under a gauge transformation (see Problem 13.12) the metric perturbations become
$$
\mathsf {h}_{\alpha \beta}^{\prime} = \mathsf {h}_{\alpha \beta} - 2 \xi_{(\alpha , \beta)} .
$$
If we contract this we see that the trace $(\mathbf{h} \equiv \mathbf{h}_{\sigma}^{\sigma} \equiv \eta^{\sigma \lambda} \mathbf{h}_{\sigma \lambda})$ transforms
as $\mathbf{h}' = \mathbf{h} - 2\xi_{, \sigma}^{\sigma}$ , (where $\xi^{\sigma} \equiv \eta^{\sigma \lambda} \xi_{\lambda}$ ). The transformation law for $\overline{\mathbf{h}}_{\alpha \beta}$ is then
$$
\begin{array}{l} \overline {{\mathrm {h}}}_{\alpha}^{\prime} \beta = \mathrm {h}_{\alpha}^{\prime} \beta - \frac {1}{2} \eta_{\alpha} \beta \mathrm {h}^{\prime} = \mathrm {h}_{\alpha} \beta - 2 \xi_{(\alpha , \beta)} - \frac {1}{2} \eta_{\alpha} \beta (\mathrm {h} - 2 \xi_{, \sigma}^{\sigma}) \\
= \overline {{\mathsf {h}}}_{\alpha \beta} - \xi_{\alpha , \beta} - \xi_{\beta , \alpha} + \eta_{\alpha \beta} \xi^{\sigma}_{, \sigma} \\
\end{array}
$$
and
$$
\begin{array}{l} \overline {{\mathfrak {h}}}_{\alpha}^{\prime} \beta^{\prime}^{\beta} = \eta^{\lambda \beta} \overline {{\mathfrak {h}}}_{\alpha}^{\prime} \beta , \lambda = \overline {{\mathfrak {h}}}_{\alpha} \beta^{\prime}^{\beta} - \xi_{\alpha ,} \beta^{\prime}^{\beta} - \xi_{\beta ,}^{\prime}^{\beta} + \xi_{\sigma ,}^{\prime}^{\sigma}, \\
= \bar {h}_{\alpha} \beta^{\prime}^{\beta} - \xi_{\alpha}, \beta^{\prime}^{\beta} = \bar {h}_{\alpha} \beta^{\prime}^{\beta} - \square \xi_{\alpha}. \\
\end{array}
$$
So we need only choose the four functions $\xi_{\alpha}$ such that they satisfy the familiar wave equation $\square \xi_{\alpha} = \overline{h}_{\alpha} \beta^{\prime \beta}$ .
Such a solution can always be found (e.g. the retarded integral of classical electromagnetic wave theory). The solution, of course, is not unique. We can always add to $\xi_{a}$ any solution of $\square \xi_{a} = 0$ .
